raw_step.causal_note: Numeric inputs from the task drove the dispatch calculation.
raw_step.observation: Computed break-even hydrogen value per MWh as 111.538461538 $/MWh. All six grid prices were below the break-even threshold, so supplementing to full 12 MW was margin-positive in every hour where renewables alone were insufficient. Hour 3 had 2 MW of renewable curtailment because available renewable supply exceeded electrolyzer capacity.
raw_step.operation: evaluate renewable-first full-capacity dispatch and per-hour costs/margins
raw_step.reasoning: The supplied economics made every grid-backed hour profitable, and the minimum-load constraint was satisfied by the full-capacity dispatch.
raw_step.state_change: Derived the full hourly dispatch, cost, revenue, margin, and curtailment values.

task: Advanced task: Hydrogen electrolyzer load-following screen. Data provided: electrolyzer_capacity = 12 MW, minimum_load = 25% (of nameplate), efficiency = 52 kWh/kg (electricity consumed per kg H2 produced), renewable_profile_MW = [3, 8, 14, 11, 5, 2] (hourly MW), grid_price = [28, 31, 65, 94, 72, 40] $/MWh (hourly), hydrogen_price = $5.80/kg. Rules: 1) use renewable energy first to meet electrolyzer input; 2) buy grid power only when producing hydrogen at a positive gross margin (electricity cost implied by grid price and efficiency vs hydrogen price); 3) avoid operating the electrolyzer below minimum_load (25% of nameplate). Produce as outputs: hourly hydrogen output (kg/h), electricity cost ($), gross margin ($ and $/kg), and curtailment (MW and MWh). Include all intermediate calculations (power allocated from renewables, grid power purchased, energy-to-hydrogen conversion, per-hour and cumulative costs), uncertainty and sensitivity notes, validation checks, and a final decision table that ties each recommendation back to the supplied inputs and rules. The deliverable must be executable from the embedded packet alone (no external data lookup).
